name: XlsxWriter (Python Excel XLSX Writer)

performance.notes: Pure Python; write-only; large files may take seconds

agent_readiness.notes: Create Excel .xlsx files in Python. Workbook, Worksheet, Format, Chart objects. write(), write_string(), write_number(), write_formula(). Cell formatting: colors, borders, fonts, number formats. Charts: bar, column, line, pie, scatter. Conditional formatting. Data validation. Sparklines. freeze_panes(). autofilter(). merge_range(). VBA macros. Images. Tables. Memory optimization for large files. Write-only (cannot read existing files — use openpyxl for read). Fast and feature-complete Excel writer.

gotchas: Write-only — cannot read or modify existing Excel files (use openpyxl)
No formula evaluation (formulas stored as strings)
Large files with many charts can be slow
VBA macro support is write-only (no debugging in Python)
best_when: Generating Excel reports from Python data
Creating formatted Excel files with charts and conditional formatting
Data export pipelines needing rich Excel formatting
Performance-critical Excel generation (faster than openpyxl for write)
avoid_when: Reading or modifying existing Excel files (use openpyxl)
summary: XlsxWriter creates Excel .xlsx files in Python with rich formatting — charts, conditional formatting, data validation, sparklines, formulas, merged cells. Write-only (read with openpyxl). Comprehensive Excel feature support. Best for generating formatted Excel reports and exports from Python. Use openpyxl when you need to both read and write existing Excel files.
